title: urtica dioica var. procera
intro: Urtica dioica var. procera, also known as tall nettle, is a variety of the stinging nettle plant, characterized by its tall stems and broad leaves. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and astringent effects, making it useful in treating various health issues such as arthritis, digestive problems, and skin conditions. The health benefits of tall nettle include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting immune function, as well as providing relief from symptoms of menopause and menstrual cramps. Tall nettle can be consumed in various herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being added to salads and used in cooking, although caution should be taken to avoid skin irritation from the stinging hairs.
